title: phymatopteris nepalensis
intro: Phymatopteris nepalensis, also known as Nepal Maidenhair, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have numerous health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antidote for snake bites. In herbal preparations, Phymatopteris nepalensis is often used in the form of decoctions, powders, or infusions, and is typically consumed as a tea or added to traditional remedies to enhance their efficacy.
